raiz.texto: Cerulea claims essential-participant status based on its monopoly on Earth communication and medical capability. Argues that operational priority and hardware contribution are irrelevant if crews cannot communicate with Earth or treat medical emergencies. Demands guaranteed full-crew presence as a non-negotiable condition, noting that its physician and communication specialists cannot be reduced without endangering all occupants. Proposes a consensus-based decision framework requiring unanimous agreement for any life support modification. States that exclusion or reduction would force it to operate its communication array from its landing vehicle for crew safety reasons, which would interrupt the habitat's connection to Earth-based mission control.
